name: COGNITION THERAPEUTICS INC
business: COGNITION THERAPEUTICS INC is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company engaged in the discovery and development of small molecule therapeutics targeting age-related degenerative diseases of the central nervous system and retina. Its lead product candidate, zervimesine (CT1812), is an orally delivered small molecule designed to protect neuronal synapses by preventing the binding of pathogenic protein oligomers. The company currently has no approved products and generates no revenue from product sales.
sellsTo: Geographic atrophy secondary to dry AMD patients

exposures.sentence: Our lead product candidate, zervimesine, also known as CT1812, is an orally delivered, small molecule designed to protect neuronal synapses by preventing the binding of oligomers of pathogenic proteins including -amyloid, or A and -synuclein.

catalysts.event: Topline results from Phase 2 START study (COG0203) in MCI and early-stage AD
catalysts.sentence: Topline results are expected after all participants have completed 18 months of treatment.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. direct healthcare costs for AD and other dementias exceed $350 billion
tamClaims.sentence: The direct healthcare costs to care for patients with AD and other dementias in the United States is estimated to exceed $350 billion.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 7 million people in the U.S. have Alzheimer's disease
tamClaims.sentence: Approximately 7 million people in the United States have been diagnosed with AD.

tamClaims.claim: Worldwide, AD affects up to 35 million people
tamClaims.sentence: The World Health Organization estimates that AD affects as many as 35 million people globally.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. DLB direct healthcare costs approximately $31 billion per year
tamClaims.sentence: According to the Lewy Body Dementia Association, the direct healthcare costs for patients with DLB are estimated to be approximately $31 billion per year.
In the United States, an estimated 1.4 million are diagnosed with DLB.

tamClaims.claim: Projected $1 trillion U.S. healthcare costs for AD/dementias by 2050
tamClaims.sentence: The direct healthcare costs to care for patients with AD and other dementias in the United States is currently estimated to exceed $350 billion and projected to increase to $1 trillion by 2050.
